mismatched_fact_examples.truth_value: Planar glide promotes continuous uniform deformation; lower SFE increases glide planarity but high strain rate impact at low temperature is difficult due to higher activation energy for slip systems, leading to limited planar glide and poor cryogenic toughness.
mismatched_fact_examples.prediction_value: Charpy impact absorbed energy (W_all): approximately 327 J at RT, 277 J at -77°C, and 188 J at -196°C (from text). Fig. 5b tabulates energy components: W_initiation ~110 J (-196°C), ~65 J (-77°C), ~55 J (RT); W_stable ~30 J (-196°C), ~180 J (-77°C), ~180 J (RT); W_unstable ~40 J (-196°C), ~50 J (-77°C), ~60 J (RT). However, text states W_initiation values as 112 J (-196°C), 68 J (-77°C), and 57 J (RT), which take precedence. W_collapse values can be inferred from the difference between W_all and the sum of other components.
mismatched_fact_examples.truth_normalized: planar glide promotes continuous uniform deformation; lower sfe increases glide planarity but high strain rate impact at low temperature is difficult due to higher activation energy for slip systems, leading to limited planar glide and poor cryogenic toughness.

mismatched_fact_examples.reason: LLM judged same-field values as not semantically matched.
